blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: I want to spend this session on emotional presence. Not physical intimacy — we'll get there if it's relevant — but this: what does it mean to you when someone is fully with you.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Eye contact. Putting the phone down. When someone is with you and you can actually feel that they're not anywhere else.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Asking the second question. When someone doesn't just accept your first answer. When they — stay in the conversation.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Do either of you feel like the other person does this.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: He does. When he's not in his head.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Kit. When you are in your head. What's in there.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Whether I'm enough. Whether what I'm building is worth anything. Whether she's going to wake up in ten years and wonder why she stayed.
Daisy didn't say anything. Her expression across the screen said everything.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: Kit, I want you to hear what you just said. That is not a thought about the present. That is a story you are telling about the future based on a wound from the past. Your father's absence is not a prophecy about yours.

blocks.speaker: Dr. Reeves
blocks.content: When you go into your head and disappear from Daisy — she is not the cause of that. But she does live inside the consequence. That's worth knowing.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I know he's not going anywhere.
She looked straight into the camera.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I know you're not going anywhere. And I need you to know I'm not either. Whatever's in your head — bring it here. That's what here is for.
He was quiet. Not the weighted kind. The kind that was trying to hold something.
